title: Hybrid Search
constraints: The retriever must combine both keyword-based and vector-based search
Exact matches on product codes and IDs must be found reliably
Semantic search must still work for natural language queries
Results from both search methods must be merged and deduplicated
hints: Implement a keyword search (e.g., BM25) alongside the vector search to catch exact term matches
Use an ensemble retriever or reciprocal rank fusion to merge results from both methods
Test with both natural language queries and exact ID lookups to verify both paths work

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI, OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-7291: Industrial-grade widget. Price: $45.99. Weight: 2.3kg."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-1148: Precision calibration tool. Price: $129.00. Accuracy: ±0.01mm."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-3364: Heavy-duty mounting bracket. Price: $18.50. Material: Stainless steel."),
    Document(page_content="All products come with a 2-year warranty and free shipping on orders over $100."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-9902: Digital multimeter with auto-ranging. Price: $89.99. Display: LCD."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)

# BUG: Vector-only search — misses exact keyword matches like product codes
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "Answer based on context.\n\nContext: {context}"),
    ("human", "{question}"),
])

def ask(question: str) -> str:
    docs = retriever.invoke(question)
    context = "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])
    chain = prompt | llm
    result = chain.invoke({"context": context, "question": question})
    return result.content

# Vector search may not find exact SKU match
print(ask("What is the price of SKU-1148?"))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai.tools import tool
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-7291: Industrial-grade widget. Price: $45.99. Weight: 2.3kg."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-1148: Precision calibration tool. Price: $129.00. Accuracy: ±0.01mm."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-3364: Heavy-duty mounting bracket. Price: $18.50. Material: Stainless steel."),
    Document(page_content="All products come with a 2-year warranty and free shipping on orders over $100."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-9902: Digital multimeter with auto-ranging. Price: $89.99. Display: LCD."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)

# BUG: Vector-only search — misses exact keyword matches like product codes
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

@tool
def search_products(query: str) -> str:
    """Search the product catalog."""
    docs = retriever.invoke(query)
    return "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])

assistant = Agent(
    role="Product Assistant",
    goal="Answer product questions based on catalog data",
    backstory="You are a helpful product assistant.",
    tools=[search_products],
)

task = Task(
    description="What is the price of SKU-1148?",
    expected_output="The price of the product",
    agent=assistant,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[assistant], tasks=[task])
print(crew.kickoff())
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-7291: Industrial-grade widget. Price: $45.99. Weight: 2.3kg."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-1148: Precision calibration tool. Price: $129.00. Accuracy: ±0.01mm."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-3364: Heavy-duty mounting bracket. Price: $18.50. Material: Stainless steel."),
    Document(page_content="All products come with a 2-year warranty and free shipping on orders over $100."),
    Document(page_content="Product SKU-9902: Digital multimeter with auto-ranging. Price: $89.99. Display: LCD."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)

# BUG: Vector-only search — misses exact keyword matches like product codes
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

@function_tool
def search_products(query: str) -> str:
    """Search the product catalog."""
    docs = retriever.invoke(query)
    return "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])

agent = Agent(
    name="Product Assistant",
    instructions="Answer product questions based on catalog data.",
    tools=[search_products],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "What is the price of SKU-1148?")
print(result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Implements keyword search
evaluationCriteria.description: A keyword-based search method (e.g., BM25) is implemented alongside vector search

evaluationCriteria.label: Finds exact term matches
evaluationCriteria.description: Product codes, SKUs, and IDs are reliably found via keyword matching

evaluationCriteria.label: Semantic search still works
evaluationCriteria.description: Natural language queries still return relevant results via vector similarity

evaluationCriteria.label: Merges results from both methods
evaluationCriteria.description: Results from keyword and vector search are combined and deduplicated
